sn5.7:1.2: Then, early in the morning, Upacālā the nun adjusted her robes and, taking her bowl & outer robe, went into Sāvatthī for alms. When she had gone for alms in Sāvatthī and had returned from her alms round, after her meal she went to the Grove of the Blind to spend the day.
sn5.7:1.3: Having gone deep into the Grove of the Blind, she sat down at the foot of a tree for the day’s abiding.
sn5.7:1.4: Then Māra the Evil One, wanting to arouse fear, horripilation, & terror in her, wanting to make her fall away from concentration, approached her & said,
sn5.7:1.5: “Where do you want to reappear [be reborn], nun?”
sn5.7:1.6: “I don’t want to reappear anywhere, my friend.”
sn5.7:3.1: Sister Upacālā: “The Devas of the Thirty-three,
sn5.7:3.5: They are bound with the bonds of sensuality;
sn5.7:3.6: they come again under Māra’s sway.
sn5.7:5.4: that’s where my heart truly delights.”
sn5.7:6.1: Then Māra the Evil One—sad & dejected at realizing, “Upacālā the nun knows me”—vanished right there.
